samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">Data width of FIFO.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">Depth of FIFO.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <para>1. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule.</para>
    <para>2. Use waddr, raddr to access dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>3. Use wenc, wdata to write data to dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>4. Use renc, raddr to read data from dual-port RAM.</para>
    <para>5. Use wptr, rptr to manage write and read pointers.</para>
    <para>6. Use wptr_buff, rptr_buff to manage write and read pointers.</para>
    <para>7. Use wfull, rempty to manage write and read full/empty signals.</para>
    <para>8. Use wptr_gray, rptr_gray to manage write and read Gray code.</para>
    <para>9. Use winc, rinc to manage write and read increment signals.</para>
    <para>10. Use wclk, rclk to manage write and read clock signals.</para>
    <para>11. Use wrstn, rrstn to manage write and read reset signals.</para>
    <para>12. Use wdata to manage write data.</para>
    <para>13. Use rdata to manage read data.</para>
    <para>14. Use wfull to manage write full signal.</para>
    <para>15. Use rempty to manage read empty signal.</para>
    </implementation>
</module>
